name: Step-1V
archDisplay: Dense multimodal transformer (LLM backbone + vision encoder), exact scale undisclosed
note: StepFun's first publicly released multimodal (vision) model, adding image understanding atop the Step-1 text base in September 2024; since deprecated in favor of newer Step vision models.

results.notes: From arXiv:2504.11101 'Consensus Entropy: Harnessing Multi-VLM Agreement for Self-Verifying and Self-Improving OCR' (Table 3, 'Representative CE Ensemble Examples On OCRBench'). The row lists four individual model scores feeding into an ensemble: 'Ovis2-1B, Qwen2.5VL-7B, Step1V, Step1o (890, 874, 886, 926)' -- Step1V's own OCRBench score is 886 (raw scale, consistent with other corpus OCRBench entries e.g. ERNIE-4.5-VL 872, InternVL2.5-78B 854). Verified consistently across three independent WebFetch reads of the paper's HTML version (arxiv.org/html/2504.11101v4, dated 06 May 2026 for v4; original submission April 2025). Unusual citation context (score appears inside an ensemble-methods table rather than a dedicated model leaderboard/report), so flagging for reviewer double-check, but the paper is a real, independently-authored academic source with Step1V's individual score explicitly stated.

verificationNotes: Confirmed real: nextomoro and other search results independently confirm Step-1V launched September 2024 as StepFun's first public multimodal model, with Step-Audio/Step-Video following in Oct/Nov 2024. Confirmed a step-1v-32k variant genuinely exists in StepFun's official model-list docs, supporting the ctxK=32 value. No result rows were provided to verify. Params left null (undisclosed) as given.
